published_items.title: Kimi K3 — Moonshot's 2.8T MoE Model Tops Frontend Code Arena, Open Weights July 27
key_facts: Kimi K3 launched July 16 on Kimi Code and Kimi app; two variants: K3 Max and K3 Swarm Max
API pricing: $3.00/$15.00 per 1M tokens; cache-hit input $0.30
Open weights scheduled for public release July 27
Frontend Code Arena: 1,679 points, #1; beats Claude Fable 5 (1,631) and GPT-5.6 Sol (1,618) in 6/7 sub-domains
Hallucination rate: 51% (up from 39% for predecessor); accuracy improved in parallel (Artificial Analysis)
Architecture: 2.8T total parameters; 1M context; sparsity ratio undisclosed
13,241 reasoning tokens per task; hidden ~85-token system prompt (simonwillison.net)
caveats: No formal Moonshot AI blog post URL indexed; architecture from third-party reporting
Moonshot has not disclosed sparsity ratio or active parameter count
Arena scores reflect community voting preferences, not production workflow performance
Artificial Analysis hallucination methodology may not generalize across all workload types
July 27 open-weight date is Moonshot-stated, not independently confirmed

published_items.title: Thinking Machines Lab Releases Inkling — 975B Open-Weight Multimodal Model
published_items.methodology: vendor primary blog + T2 trade press
key_facts: Inkling released July 15; 975B total / 41B active MoE parameters; 1M context; 45T training tokens
Weights at thinkingmachines/inkling on Hugging Face
API via TogetherAI, Fireworks, Modal, Databricks, Baseten
Inkling-Small: 276B/12B, preview release same date
Tinker platform: 64K/256K context options; 50% introductory discount
Founded by Mira Murati (former OpenAI CTO); ~200 employees; trained on Nvidia GB300 NVL72
Company explicitly states Inkling is 'not the strongest overall model available today'
caveats: No independent benchmark results published at launch; Arena.ai and Artificial Analysis scores not yet available
Tinker pricing details not disclosed in launch post
Company headcount and GPU infrastructure from TechCrunch reporting, not independently verified
1M-token context may not be available on all hosting partners

published_items.title: Claude Mid-Conversation System Messages Exit Beta on Fable 5, Mythos 5, Opus 4.8
key_facts: Beta header requirement removed; feature GA on Claude API, Amazon Bedrock, Google Cloud Vertex
Available on Claude Fable 5, Claude Mythos 5, Claude Opus 4.8; not on Claude Sonnet 5
System message must immediately follow a user or tool-result turn; other placements return 400 error
Cache prefix stays intact when system message is appended mid-conversation
Documented use cases: permission changes, token-budget updates, mode switches, agentic loop mid-session input
caveats: Primary Anthropic release notes at support.claude.com do not list this update in Jul 14-17 range
July 15 date is from Releasebot (T3) only; exact date of beta-to-GA transition lacks primary Anthropic announcement
Feature confirmed available without beta header by Anthropic's official documentation

published_items.title: Claude Code Ships Broad Agentic Session and Stability Update
key_facts: Subagent session fix: returning to agent no longer silently drops subagents and re-runs prompt from scratch
Context-usage indicator CPU and memory regression fixed
Background agent PATH inheritance fix on Windows
macOS 15-20 second stall fix on background agent session open/switch
--forward-subagent-text flag added for stream-json subagent output; CLAUDE_CODE_FORWARD_SUBAGENT_TEXT env var

published_items.title: Gemini 3.5 Pro Misses Third Target Date; Stopgap 3.6 Flash Reported
key_facts: No gemini-3.5-pro model ID in Gemini API changelog; most recent entry July 6, 2026
Third target date (July 17) missed; original target June 2026, prior delay to earlier July
Issues cited: hallucinations and inconsistent outputs
Potential stopgap gemini-3.6-flash and gemini-3.5-flash-light model IDs registered
Unconfirmed pre-release specs: 2M context, $1.25/$10.00 per million tokens, Deep Think on Ultra tier
caveats: Primary source for delay is Geeky Gadgets (T3); Google has published no official communication
API changelog absence (last entry July 6) is most reliable signal, but there may be lag between launch and update
If Gemini 3.5 Pro launched later on July 17 after this run, item will require correction

published_items.title: Xi Jinping Opens WAIC 2026; China Advances WAICO as Rival AI Governance Body
key_facts: Xi Jinping delivered opening keynote at WAIC 2026 in Shanghai July 17; first in-person head-of-state appearance
State Council described speech as elaborating China's policies and positions on AI development and governance
Proposed deliverables: 'China Wisdom for the World' Case Collection (20+ countries), Action Plan on AI Cooperation
WAICO (World AI Cooperation Organization) advanced as multilateral governance body headquartered in Shanghai
WAICO first proposed by Premier Li Qiang at WAIC 2025
Xi keynote is highest-profile head-of-state engagement at a dedicated AI governance summit to date
caveats: Speech content described from pre-speech state media briefings (July 13-16); actual transcript not yet indexed
WAICO remains a Chinese diplomatic proposal; no participating countries or charter published
Xi's specific framing on chip sanctions and US tech restrictions based on analyst previews, not confirmed speech text
